blurb: q, g', x and o' — made further back than English goes

sections.heading: Four sounds English doesn't have
body: Most of Uzbek is friendly to an English mouth — no tones, no clicks, and you say what you see. Four letters need real practice: **q**, **g'**, **x** and the vowel **o'**. All of them live further back in the throat than English ever goes.

**q** is a deep “k” from where you'd gargle (Samar**q**and). **g'** is a gargled “gh”, like a French r (tog' — mountain). **x** is the ch in Scottish *loch* (ya**x**shi — good). **o'** is a rounded vowel like British *more*, clipped short (o'zbek).

examples.english: mountain — the gargled g' at the end

sections.heading: They change meaning
body: These aren't accents on familiar sounds — they're **different letters** that change the word. *bor* means “there is”; *bo'r* means “chalk”. Collapse q into k or x into h and you may be saying something else entirely.
Two habits help most: keep every vowel **full and clear** (no English mumbling), and aim q, g' and x at the very back of your throat.

exercises.instruction: The “kh” sound in yaxshi (like Scottish loch) is written with which letter?

options.explain: h is a plain breathy English h — a different letter.
k is a normal front k. The loch sound is x.

exercises.instruction: Which letter makes the deep, back-of-the-throat “k” in Samarqand?

options.explain: q is uvular — as far back as you can manage.
k is the ordinary front k — q sits much deeper.
g' is the voiced gargle — q's noisy cousin.

exercises.instruction: Listen to tog' (mountain). The final g' sound is…

options.text: a gargled “gh”, like a French r
options.explain: Exactly — a continuous guttural sound, not a hard stop.
A hard g is plain g. g' keeps the air flowing — it gargles.
Nothing is silent in Uzbek — every letter speaks.

options.text: are two different words — the vowel matters
options.explain: bor = “there is”, bo'r = “chalk”. o and o' are separate letters.

options.text: are the same word, spelled two ways
options.explain: Tempting, but no — o' is its own vowel, and it changes the meaning.

wrapUp: q, g', x and o' are real letters with real jobs. Aim for the back of your throat and Samarqand, tog' and yaxshi will come out right.
